name: ai-voice-input
title: AI Voice Input
description: Press-to-hold or toggle recording control with idle, recording, paused, transcribing, ready, and error states, elapsed duration, an explicit discard, and a visible statement of where the audio currently lives.

props.name: state
props.description: Controlled state. Every transition belongs to the caller.

props.name: handling
props.description: Required. Where the audio physically is right now — only the caller knows whether transcription runs on-device.

props.name: handlingLabel
props.description: Override the handling notice copy, e.g. to name the transcription vendor or the agreement covering it.

props.name: duration
props.description: Elapsed recording time in seconds. Caller-owned clock.

props.name: maxDuration
props.description: Recording cap in seconds. Drives the progress bar when set.

props.name: mode
props.description: Click to toggle, or press and hold to record.

props.name: transcript
props.description: The transcript, shown for review in the ready state.

props.name: error
props.description: What went wrong, shown in the error state.

props.name: onDiscard
props.description: Discards the audio and any transcript. Offered whenever either exists.

props.name: onInsert
props.description: Accept the reviewed transcript into the composer.

clinicalNotes: Dictation is the point at which a consulting room becomes a recording, and clinicians routinely misjudge where that recording goes. `handling` is therefore required and always rendered: the component will not let you ship a microphone button that stays silent about whether the audio is still on the device, in flight to a vendor, or already reduced to text. Say it in the caller's own words — name the processor and the agreement — because "secure" means nothing to a patient who can be identified from thirty seconds of audio. The transcript is shown for review before it enters the conversation, never inserted for the clinician: speech-to-text mishears drug names and doses in exactly the ways that matter (fifteen for fifty, hydralazine for hydroxyzine), so the accept action is a deliberate human act and discard is offered at every stage that holds audio. Recording, permission, and transcription are all caller-owned; this component captures nothing.
notes: Hold mode wires pointer capture plus Space/Enter keydown/keyup so it is operable from the keyboard. Progress is reused as the duration meter and, while transcribing, as a pulsing indeterminate bar.
